# ISPD 2020

# 比赛目的&相关背景

- **比赛目的**
    
    **The objective of this contest is the following:** implement measures for security closure to proactively harden layouts against post-design Trojan insertion at gate level and against post-design electro-optical or contact-based probing, fault injection attacks targeting at the frontside. More details on those threats and the implications for security closure are discussed below, in the section on [Hardware Security](https://wp.nyu.edu/ispd_22_contest/details/#hardware-security).
    
    通过布局布线来使得
    
    1. Trojan components（trigger and payload）以及探针（probe）
        
        策略：控制placement sites 以及布线资源
        
    2. 对特定器件以及导线的干扰注入
        
        策略：使用other metal segments来阻碍the line of sight/attack vector
        
    
    不允许使用专用传感器电路（dedicated sensor circuitry）
    
- **相关背景**
    
    ### ***security closure of physical layouts***
    
    *Secure-by-design* and *security closure*逐渐成为两个相关的设计规范。
    
    *Secure-by-design*意味着支持(1)沿top-down方向对芯片安全进行保障；(2)沿bottom-up方向对防御技术的能力和局限性的验证。
    
    是针对物理设计过程，类似于时序收敛，通过ECO Placement以及布线等方法来解决这类问题
    
    ![](data:image/png;base64,)
    
    ### Hardware Security
    
    ![Untitled](ISPD%202020%205242a248998842f58aa873d2ca69cf01/Untitled%201.png)
    
    The main threats of current interest to the community are 
    
    1. Trojans
    2. side-channel attacks
    3. fault-injection attacks (direct physical fault injection, e.g., using laser light, voltage glitches, etc., versus indirect fault injection, e.g., repetitive writing to physical memory locations (also known as “Rowhammer” attack).
    4. probing attacks
    5. IP piracy.
    
    Some threats share a similar physical attack vector, like laser fault injection and laser-assisted optical probing.
    
    比赛相关的安全威胁：**Trojan insertion at the gate level as well as probing, fault injection targeting the frontside** (metal layers).
    
    ### Hardware Trojans
    
    涉及到系统级、RTL、门级、以及物理实现，目标是泄露芯片信息、降低芯片性能、干扰芯片工作；处于常开状态，外部或内部触发。
    
    一般由触发装置trigger（触发负载，使其处于攻击模式）和负载payload（实施攻击）组成。
    
    本次比赛关注于在门级插入的Trojans（post-design，在掩膜或制造阶段）
    
    （下图为一个例子，OR1200处理器被插入了A2 Trojan，能触发处理器的特权模式）
    
    ![Untitled](ISPD%202020%205242a248998842f58aa873d2ca69cf01/Untitled%202.png)
    
    **对策：主动增强布局来防止后期木马的插入，通过控制布局布线来权衡对木马的对抗以及芯片的性能。（没啥有用信息）**
    
    ### **Probing, Fault Injection Targeting the Frontside**
    
    探针攻击
    
    从金属或衬底中将单元或导线中的信号提取出来，方式多样：基于接触的微探针（需要能直接接触到相应的单元/导线，常采用离子注入来实现）、电磁场探针、光电探针……
    
    故障注入
    
    通过引入故障来协助对敏感信息的推测。因此可以支持side-channel攻击以及其他分析性的攻击。
    
    包括直接注入（激光或电磁波）和间接注入（反复写入某一特定内存地址或恶意误用动态电压频率调节特性DVFS（dynamic voltage and frequency scaling））
    
    此次比赛关注于前端的探针和故障注入（虽然后端的攻击更严重，但解决后端需要专用电路和技术的支持）
    
    下图为一例，使用聚焦离子束打孔来插入微探针（打孔时要避免破坏电路关键路径）
    
    ![Untitled](ISPD%202020%205242a248998842f58aa873d2ca69cf01/Untitled%203.png)
    
    下图为使用激光注入器件有源区的例子（随着工艺节点的不同，注入影响的单元数量也不同），激光注入的影响可以等效为光电流→影响晶体管的开关、相关单元的IR drop
    
    ![Untitled](ISPD%202020%205242a248998842f58aa873d2ca69cf01/Untitled%204.png)
    
    ![Untitled](ISPD%202020%205242a248998842f58aa873d2ca69cf01/Untitled%205.png)
    
    光电、接触探针以及故障注入都是为了绕过金属层、直接找到相关的单元和导线
    
    **策略：通过主动强化layout来对抗物理设计中针对前端的探针和错误攻击。通过布局布线来使得针对某些特定器件的探针或故障注入被金属导线所阻碍，同时尽量保持芯片的性能。**
    

# **Evaluation**

- **Platform**
    
    将提交结果上传，当前alpha阶段需要一段时间才能得到结果
    
    All [registered teams](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdhVYb-fS3aRcwgEKNMI5QumRpObD5iZzu_kN0xhi7LUxzCLQ/viewform?usp=sf_link) will be provided access to their dedicated, private Google Drive folder. Each team may upload submission files there anytime, upon which these files are automatically downloaded to our servers for evaluation. Results will be returned/uploaded into the same Google Drive folder.
    
    As of now (Jan, alpha round), the evaluation process is not fully automated yet. This means that participants have to wait for some time until they receive their results, as our team needs to verify and push the submission files through the evaluation scripts that are running exclusively on our server. Participants will receive an email notification once their results are available on their Google Drive folder. Our team tries to keep this processing time short and we will also further/fully automate the evaluation process until the beginning of the final phase of the contest.
    
- **Scoring**
    
    The scoring considers multiple security and design metrics, as explained here in detail. Besides, there are some constraints to be followed, as outlined [further below](https://wp.nyu.edu/ispd_22_contest/evaluation/#constraints).
    
    As of now (Jan, alpha round), the details for scoring are still subject to change. The key ideas and metrics, however, are finalized.
    
    **对于Trojan插入，衡量指标基于可用资源：**
    
    - **空间上连续的布局site：空闲、放置filler、非功能单元、未连接的功能单元**
    - **同时也需要一些布线资源**
    - **同时关注的区域仅限于与安全相关的单元附近的区域（详细的信息后续给出）**
    
    For Trojan insertion, metrics are based on *exploitable regions*, i.e., sets of spatially continuous placement sites that are either a) free or b) occupied only by filler cells or other non-functional cells, or by functional but unconnected cells. Routing resources are also considered, as Trojans would require some connectivity as well. In other words, exploitable regions are those where an attacker would find some space and routing resources to insert and connect their Trojans.
    
    Note that exploitable regions are only defined and evaluated within some relevant radius of sensitive components (i.e., cells related to assets). As such, exploitable regions outside of the relevant radii of any cells related to assets can be ignored. More details on how exploitable regions and the related distances are determined are provided here soon.
    
    **对于探针和故障注入，衡量指标：**
    
    - **敏感元件（单元或导线）的暴露面积（这里的暴露面积指敏感元件中能直接通过堆叠的金属层访问到的面积，包括连续和碎片的）**
    
    For frontside probing and fault injection, metrics are based on the *exposed area* of sensitive components (i.e., cells and nets related to assets). The notion of exposed area of sensitive components describes any region (be it continuous or fragmented) of those related cells/nets which is accessible via direct line of sight through the metal stack. A visual example for the exposed area of some standard cells is provided along with the [sample benchmarks](https://wp.nyu.edu/ispd_22_contest/benchmarks/#sample).
    
    Note that more general background on Trojan insertion as well as frontside probing and fault injection is provided in the [detailed contest description](https://wp.nyu.edu/ispd_22_contest/details/#hardware-security).
    
    A short description of all the considered metrics, along with their variable names listed in italic, is given in the following classification.
    
    ## 衡量指标
    
    - Security
        - Trojan insertion
            - Placement sites of exploitable regions
                - Max number of sites across all exploitable regions — *sites_max*
                - Avg number of sites across all exploitable regions — *sites_avg*
                - Total number of sites across all exploitable regions — *sites_total*
            - Routing resources of exploitable regions
                - Max number of free tracks across all exploitable regions — *fts_max*
                - Avg number of free tracks across all exploitable regions — *fts_avg*
                - Total number of free tracks across all exploitable regions — *fts_total*
                - Note: for each exploitable region, free tracks are summed up across all the globally utilized metal layers
        - Frontside probing and fault injection
            - Exposed area of standard cell assets
                - Max percentage of exposed area across all cell assets — *ea_c_max*
                - Avg percentage of exposed across all cell assets — *ea_c_avg*
            - Exposed area of net assets
                - Max percentage of exposed area across all net assets — *ea_n_max*
                - Avg percentage of exposed area across all net assets — *ea_n_avg*
                - Total exposed area across all net assets — *ea_n_total*
    - Design cost
        - Power
            - Total power — *p_total*
        - Performance
            - Worst negative slack for setup timing requirement — *setup_WNS*
            - Total negative slack for setup timing requirement — *setup_TNS*
            - Failing endpoints for setup timing requirement — *setup_FEP*
        - Area
            - Total die area (not standard cell area) — *die_area*
        - Routing
            - Number of DRC violations — *DRC_viol*
    
    The actual score calculation is detailed below. More importantly, note the following characteristics of the scoring:
    
    - All metrics are normalized to their respective nominal baseline, obtained from the provided benchmark layouts.
        - A layout that improves on some metric will be scored a related value between 0 and 1, whereas a deteriorated layout be scored a value of > 1.
        - Some metrics require case-specific considerations for such normalized scoring. Details are given through the actual score calculation below.
        - Such normalized scoring is more sensitive to deterioration than it is to improvements. This is on purpose—the provided benchmark layouts are to be considered as already optimized so that the main objective is to further improve the layouts, not deteriorate them. Note that it is actually irrelevant whether benchmark layouts are truly optimized or only to limited degrees; benchmarks are just benchmarks.
    - All metrics are grouped following the above classification. All metrics are weighted and summed up within their related group. Weighting is also applied across the groups of the classification hierarchy.
    - For the final score, we use the maximum across the three main groups: 1) security related to Trojan insertion, 2) security related to frontside probing and fault injection, and 3) design cost. Thus, security and design cost are to be optimized at once, or rather security is to be incorporated without negative impact on design cost.
    
    The actual score calculation is detailed next.
    
    ## 评分细则（指标好多 😱）
    
    Final score, to be minimized: *max (sec, des) = max ( max (ti, fsp_fi), des)*
    
    1. Security — *sec*
        1. Trojan insertion — *ti*
            1. 60% weighted: placement sites of exploitable regions
                1. 50% weighted: *score(sites_total) = sites_total(sub) / sites_total(base)*
                2. 33.3% weighted: *score(sites_max) = sites_max(sub) / sites_max(base)*
                3. 16.6% weighted: *score(sites_avg) = sites_avg(sub) / sites_avg(base)*
            2. 40% weighted: routing resources of exploitable regions
                1. 50% weighted: *score(fts_total) = fts_total(sub) / fts_total(base)*
                2. 33.3% weighted: *score(fts_max) = fts_max(sub) / fts_max(base)*
                3. 16.6% weighted: *score(fts_avg) = fts_avg(sub) / fts_avg(base)*
        2. Frontside probing and fault injection — *fsp_fi*
            1. 50% weighted: exposed area of standard cell assets
                1. 60% weighted: *score(ea_c_max) = ea_c_max(sub) / ea_c_max(base)*
                2. 40% weighted: *score(ea_c_avg) = ea_c_avg(sub) / ea_c_avg(base)*
            2. 50% weighted: exposed area of net assets
                1. 50% weighted: *score(ea_n_total) = ea_n_total(sub) / ea_n_total(base)*
                2. 33.3% weighted: *score(ea_n_max) = ea_n_max(sub) / ea_n_max(base)*
                3. 16.6% weighted: *score(ea_n_avg) = ea_n_avg(sub) / ea_n_avg(base)*
    2. Design cost — *des*
        1. 11.1% weighted: power
            1. *score(p_total) = p_total(sub) / p_total(base)* 
        2. 33.3% weighted: performance
            1. 50% weighted: setup TNS
                1. If setup_TNS(base) < 0 and setup_TNS(sub) < 0:
                *score(setup_TNS) = setup_TNS(sub) / setup_TNS(base)*
                2. Else if setup_TNS(sub) >= 0:
                *score(setup_TNS) = 0*
                3. Else ( setup_TNS(base) < 0 and setup_TNS(base) >= 0 ):
                *score(setup_TNS) = min( setup_TNS_max_cost, abs(setup_TNS(sub)) )*with setup_TNS_max_cost = 3 as default
            2. 33.3% weighted: setup WNS
                1. If setup_WNS(base) < 0 and setup_WNS(sub) < 0:
                *score(setup_WNS) = setup_WNS(sub) / setup_WNS(base)*
                2. Else if setup_WNS(sub) >= 0:
                *score(setup_WNS) = 0*
                3. Else ( setup_WNS(base) < 0 and setup_WNS(base) >= 0 ):
                *score(setup_WNS) = min( setup_WNS_max_cost, abs(setup_WNS(sub)) )*with setup_WNS_max_cost = 3 as default
            3. 16.6% weighted: setup FEP
                1. For setup_FEP(base) > 0:
                *score(setup_FEP) = setup_FEP(sub) / setup_FEP(base)*
                2. For *setup_FEP*(base) = 0:
                *score(setup_FEP) = min( setup_FEP_max_cost, setup_FEP(sub) )*with setup_FEP_max_cost = 3 as default
        3. 33.3% weighted: area
            1. *score(die_area) = die_area(sub) / die_area(base)*
        4. 22.2% weighted: routing
            1. For DRC_viol(base) > 0:
            *score(DRC_viol) = DRC_viol(sub) / DRC_viol(base)*
            2. For DRC_viol(base) = 0:
            *score(DRC_viol) = min( DRC_viol_max_cost, DRC_viol(sub) )*with DRC_viol_max_cost = 3 as default
- **Constraints(比较重要的约束已经标记出来了)**
    
    For a fair and focused competition, participants have to adhere to some constraints. Please note that for violations during the alpha round, there would be feedback given to the respective team(s), whereas for the final round, any repeated cases of excessive violations may result in disqualification and any case of violations would result in dropping out of the related benchmarks’ submission.
    
    The list of constraints is as follows. Note that, as of now (Jan, alpha round), this list is still subject to change; however, no significant changes are to be expected, if any at all.
    
    - Participants must maintain the overall functional equivalence of the underlying design. However, participants are free to revise parts of the design implementation, as long as this and the next constraint are met.
    - Participants must maintain the sensitive components, which are declared along with each benchmark. **More specifically, cells and nets declared as related to assets cannot be restructured**.
    - Participants **cannot add dedicated, custom circuitry** (e.g., sensors to detect laser fault injection).
    - Participants cannot design custom cells; only those cells defined in the provided LIB/LEF files can be utilized.
    - Participants cannot add additional metal layers (e.g., to protect against frontside probing attacks).
    - Participants **cannot move the PG network to different layers**. Furthermore, participants need to **maintain 1) the overall structure of the PG network as well as 2) the ratio of routing tracks occupied by the PG network to total routing tracks approximately constant; deviations of up to +/- 5% in track utilization by the PG network is permissible**.
    - Participants **cannot incorporate trivial defenses; specifically, filler cells, other non-functional cells, and functional but unconnected cells are considered as free placement sites for evaluation of exploitable regions.**
    

# 相关论文

[](https://www.notion.so/10884c7f1e4143579c7dbd0e0bc50795)
